layman_headline: Market fear is rising and everything's dropping — patience is the play right now.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is in a broad sell-off driven by a global risk-off mood, with AI stock selloffs bleeding into crypto and geopolitical tension from Iran adding pressure. Bitcoin slipped below $63,000 and is dragging altcoins down with it. Fear is elevated at 27/100 and BTC dominance at 56.2% means money is leaving altcoins. While some coins are approaching attractive oversold levels, nothing has confirmed a bottom yet — catching a falling knife here is premature.
layman_action: Stay on the sidelines for now. If Ethereum dips toward $1,700-$1,750 and shows a clear bounce with rising volume, that could be the entry. Don't rush — the fear index is improving slightly but the broader market hasn't stabilized yet.
expert_reasoning: Scanning the candidates: BONK is in freefall (-22% weekly) with a -1.4 chart bias and bearish marubozu — classic falling knife. DEXE has beautiful chart structure but RSI at 85 screams overbought, making entry here extremely risky. HYPE dropped 12.8% in a week but RSI at 59 isn't oversold enough for a mean-reversion play. LDO surged 23% in 7 days and despite low RSI, it's likely to retrace after such a sharp move. ETH stands out with RSI at 35 (our highest-conviction signal type at 80% win rate), bullish marubozu candle pattern, and the most liquid market. However, price is below all major moving averages with a neutral chart bias of 0.0 — the structure hasn't turned bullish yet. The macro headwinds (chip rout, Iran tension, BTC weakness) make this a wait-for-confirmation environment. I'm targeting ETH at $1,750 for a potential entry if we see a hammer or morning star reversal candle with volume.
risk_factors: Global risk-off sentiment could intensify — Iran-US escalation or further AI stock selloff could push BTC to $60K and ETH below $1,700
BTC dominance at 56.2% and rising means altcoins including ETH could underperform even if BTC stabilizes
ETH is below all major moving averages — bearish structure could lead to further breakdown toward $1,600
watch_list: BTC price action around the $62,000 support level — a break below could trigger cascading liquidations
Fear & Greed index trajectory — it's improving from 25 to 27; a move above 30 would signal stabilization
ETH RSI dropping below 30 with a bullish candle reversal pattern — that would be the ideal entry trigger
market_context: Broad risk-off sentiment is gripping markets as AI stock selloffs, geopolitical tensions from Iran, and a global chip rout spill over into crypto. Bitcoin is below $63K with Fear at 27/100, and altcoins are bleeding harder with BTC dominance pushing 56.2%.
context_banner: Market-wide selloff driven by macro risk-off — patience required until a clear reversal signal emerges on quality assets like ETH.

key_signals.description: ETH RSI at 35 is approaching oversold territory — our most reliable signal (80% win rate) — but hasn't triggered the sub-30 level that would give highest conviction for entry.
Bitcoin price sagging under $62.5K as Iran strikes compound US stock pressure, confirming a multi-front risk-off environment that argues for patience over aggression.
Risk-off wave dragging BTC below $63K as AI selloff spreads from stocks to crypto — macro headwinds are real and not yet priced in.
Fear at 27 improving from 25 — the trend is slightly better but still deep in fear territory, historically good for accumulation but premature without technical confirmation.
ETH showing bullish marubozu candle but price remains below all major MAs with neutral chart bias — conflicting signals warrant waiting for clearer structure.
